response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web software engineer with 15+ years across TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, but the role requires deep hardware performance architecture expertise — SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator optimization, GPU workload analysis, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears in the resume. Aside from brief C/C++ work at National Instruments and low-latency service development, there is no evidence of chip-level modeling, deep-learning workload optimization, or SIMD/MIMD architecture experience. The profile is a strong senior application developer, not a principal performance architect.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience meeting the BS + 8 years bar
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching in DLang at millions of requests/sec)
Some systems-level and hardware-adjacent exposure (C/C++ at National Instruments, embedded and robotics tooling)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or deep-learning training/inference optimization background
Career trajectory centers on web/full-stack product development, not principal-level performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No AI/DL workload or GPU experience
Strong general engineering tenure and systems performance work
